# On Your Feet

> A dead-simple stand-up reminder and desk break timer for office workers. Start a timer (30, 45, or 60 minutes); when it ends, take a 5-minute walking break; repeat. Backed by 2023 Columbia research (Duran/Diaz et al.) showing 5-minute light walking every 30 minutes reduces post-meal blood sugar and blood pressure.

The site is a static SvelteKit app with no accounts, no tracking, and no ads. It is available in 13 languages with URL-prefix routing (e.g., `/en/`, `/fr/`, `/ja/`). The content is identical across languages — only the UI labels and the Why page prose differ. Source code is open under AGPL-3.0.

## Pages — English (canonical)

- [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/en/): The timer interface — pick a duration and start
- [Why this exists](https://onyourfeet.app/en/why): The peer-reviewed research behind short walking breaks, with full citations and honest caveats

## Pages — other languages

- Spanish (Español): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/es/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/es/why)
- Portuguese (Português, Brazilian): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/pt/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/pt/why)
- French (Français): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/fr/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/fr/why)
- German (Deutsch): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/de/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/de/why)
- Italian (Italiano): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/it/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/it/why)
- Dutch (Nederlands): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/nl/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/nl/why)
- Polish (Polski): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/pl/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/pl/why)
- Russian (Русский): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/ru/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/ru/why)
- Japanese (日本語): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/ja/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/ja/why)
- Korean (한국어): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/ko/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/ko/why)
- Simplified Chinese (中文): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/zh/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/zh/why)
- Hindi (हिन्दी): [Home](https://onyourfeet.app/hi/) · [Why](https://onyourfeet.app/hi/why)

Non-English Why pages show an inline notice flagging that the prose is machine-translated and pointing readers to the English original as the source of truth.

## Source code and releases

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/jtwebman/onyourfeet): AGPL-3.0-licensed source
- [Releases and changelog](https://github.com/jtwebman/onyourfeet/releases): Version history with notes on every change since launch

## Key research the Why page cites

- Duran AT, Friel CP, Serafini MA, Ensari I, Cheung YK, Diaz KM. *Breaking Up Prolonged Sitting to Improve Cardiometabolic Risk: Dose-Response Analysis of a Randomized Crossover Trial.* Medicine & Science in Sports & Exercise. 2023;55(5):847–855. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36728338/)
- Ekelund U, Steene-Johannessen J, Brown WJ, et al. *Does physical activity attenuate, or even eliminate, the detrimental association of sitting time with mortality? A harmonised meta-analysis of data from more than 1 million men and women.* The Lancet. 2016;388(10051):1302–1310. [PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27475271/)
- Bull FC, Al-Ansari SS, Biddle S, et al. *World Health Organization 2020 guidelines on physical activity and sedentary behaviour.* British Journal of Sports Medicine. 2020;54(24):1451–1462. [PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7719906/)

## What it deliberately is not

This app is not a Pomodoro timer, productivity tracker, fitness app, or scheduling tool. It does not gamify breaks, score the user, or push a wellness program. It implements one specific evidence-based intervention — a 30/45/60-minute work cycle followed by a 5-minute walking break — with no other features.
